title: zea mays subsp. indentata
intro: Zea mays subsp. indentata, also known as wahoo or American wahoo, is a plant species in the corn family that is native to eastern North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve pain. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and sore throats, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Zea mays subsp. indentata, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, have been used to promote digestive health and alleviate respiratory problems.
